topic.what: LLM-driven malware detection using retrieval-augmented generation on assembly code
topic.how: Converts assembly functions to semantic embeddings via Qwen3, builds searchable FAISS knowledge base, uses density-weighted anchor selection to identify malicious code and generate forensic explanations
topic.why_matters: Addresses black-box limitation of deep learning detectors by providing verifiable forensic evidence rather than opaque probabilistic verdicts; resists syntactic obfuscation by operating on semantic manifold rather than surface statistics

characteristics.context: Chronological split, validation on held-out samples post June 2023
Strict constraint: FPR on benign < 1% during threshold optimization
Source-level equivalents from Comet/Zeus under O0 to O3 and Os optimization

characteristics.what_it_measures: FAISS ANN search time per function
characteristics.context: 1,000 function binary; primary bottleneck is encoding step

characteristics.what_it_measures: Verified claim support rate in generated explanations
characteristics.context: Hallucination audit on Dsrc ground truth: 2.1% fabricated references

good_for: Tier-2 SOC analysis with GPU infrastructure
Detecting obfuscated variants of known families
requires: Pre-indexed knowledge base of known malware

novelty: First assembly-level RAG framework for malware detection
Density-weighted anchor selection mechanism for explainability
Semantic library filtering in embedding space
Forensic explainability via semantic manifold alignment vs classification
open_problems: Cold start problem: struggles with zero-days lacking semantic overlap to KB
Static analysis barrier: fails against virtualization obfuscation (VMProtect, Themida)
Computational throughput vs latency tradeoff: encoding step is GPU-bound bottleneck
Hallucination risk: 2.1% fabricated algorithm attributions in generative explanations
